heading: Requirements to identify and control the export of emerging and foundational technologies
label: 4817 Requirements to identify and control the export of emerging and foundational technologies

children.label: A
children.text: are essential to the national security of the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: are not critical technologies described in clauses (i) through (v) of .section 4565(a)(6)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: publicly available information;

children.label: ii
children.text: classified information, including relevant information provided by the Director of National Intelligence;

children.label: iii
children.text: information relating to reviews and investigations of transactions by the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States under ; andsection 4565 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: information provided by the advisory committees established by the Secretary to advise the Under Secretary of Commerce for Industry and Security on controls under the Export Administration Regulations, including the Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee;

children.label: i
children.text: the development of emerging and foundational technologies in foreign countries;

children.label: ii
children.text: the effect export controls imposed pursuant to this section may have on the development of such technologies in the United States; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the effectiveness of export controls imposed pursuant to this section on limiting the proliferation of emerging and foundational technologies to foreign countries; and

children.label: C
children.text: include a notice and comment period.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except to the extent inconsistent with the authorities described in subsection (a)(1)(B), the Secretary shall establish appropriate controls under the Export Administration Regulations on the export, reexport, or in-country transfer of technology identified pursuant to subsection (a), including through interim controls (such as by informing a person that a license is required for export), as appropriate, or by publishing additional regulations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may, in coordination with the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of State, and the heads of other Federal agencies, as appropriate, specify the level of control to apply under paragraph (1) with respect to the export of technology described in that paragraph, including a requirement for a license or other authorization for the export, reexport, or in-country transfer of that technology.

children.label: i
children.text: lists of countries to which exports from the United States are restricted; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the potential end uses and end users of the technology.

children.heading: Minimum requirements
children.label: C Minimum requirements
children.text: At a minimum, except as provided by paragraph (4), the Secretary shall require a license for the export, reexport, or in-country transfer of technology described in paragraph (1) to or in a country subject to an embargo, including an arms embargo, imposed by the United States.

children.heading: Procedures
children.label: A Procedures
children.text: The procedures set forth in Executive Order 12981 ( note; relating to administration of export controls) or a successor order shall apply to the review of an application for a license or other authorization for the export, reexport, or in-country transfer of technology described in paragraph (1).50 U.S.C. 4603

children.heading: Consideration of information relating to national security
children.label: B Consideration of information relating to national security
children.text: In reviewing an application for a license or other authorization for the export, reexport, or in-country transfer of technology described in paragraph (1), the Secretary shall take into account information provided by the Director of National Intelligence regarding any threat to the national security of the United States posed by the proposed export, reexport, or transfer. The Director of National Intelligence shall provide such information on the request of the Secretary.

children.heading: Disclosures relating to collaborative arrangements
children.label: C Disclosures relating to collaborative arrangements
children.text: In the case of an application for a license or other authorization for the export, reexport, or in-country transfer of technology described in paragraph (1) submitted by or on behalf of a joint venture, joint development agreement, or similar collaborative arrangement, the Secretary may require the applicant to identify, in addition to any foreign person participating in the arrangement, any foreign person with significant ownership interest in a foreign person participating in the arrangement.

children.label: i
children.text: described in ; orsection 1702(b) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: if the regulation of the export of that technology is prohibited under any other provision of law.

children.heading: Regulatory exceptions
children.label: B Regulatory exceptions
children.text: In prescribing regulations under paragraph (1), the Secretary may include regulatory exceptions to the requirements of that paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: The sale or license of a finished item and the provision of associated technology if the United States person that is a party to the transaction generally makes the finished item and associated technology available to its customers, distributors, or resellers.

children.label: ii
children.text: The sale or license to a customer of a product and the provision of integration services or similar services if the United States person that is a party to the transaction generally makes such services available to its customers.

children.label: iii
children.text: The transfer of equipment and the provision of associated technology to operate the equipment if the transfer could not result in the foreign person using the equipment to produce critical technologies (as defined in ).section 4565(a) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: The procurement by the United States person that is a party to the transaction of goods or services, including manufacturing services, from a foreign person that is a party to the transaction, if the foreign person has no rights to exploit any technology contributed by the United States person other than to supply the procured goods or services.

children.label: v
children.text: Any contribution and associated support by a United States person that is a party to the transaction to an industry organization related to a standard or specification, whether in development or declared, including any license of or commitment to license intellectual property in compliance with the rules of any standards organization (as defined by the Secretary by regulation).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of State, in consultation with the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense, and the heads of other Federal agencies, as appropriate, shall propose that any technology identified pursuant to subsection (a) be added to the list of technologies controlled by the relevant multilateral export control regimes.

children.heading: Items on commerce control list or United States munitions list
children.label: 2 Items on commerce control list or United States munitions list
children.text: If the Secretary of State proposes to a multilateral export control regime under paragraph (1) to add a technology identified pursuant to subsection (a) to the control list of that regime and that regime does not add that technology to the control list during the 3-year period beginning on the date of the proposal, the applicable agency head may determine whether national security concerns warrant the continuation of unilateral export controls with respect to that technology.

children.heading: Report to Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States
children.label: d Report to Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States
children.text: Not less frequently than every 180 days, the Secretary, in coordination with the Secretary of Defense, the Secretary of State, and the heads of other Federal agencies, as appropriate, shall submit to the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States a report on the results of actions taken pursuant to this section.
the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs, the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on Armed Services, and the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate; and
the Committee on Financial Services, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Armed Services, and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Modifications to Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee
children.label: f Modifications to Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall revise the objectives of the Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee, established by the Secretary under the Export Administration Regulations, to include advising the interagency process established under subsection (a) with respect to emerging and foundational technologies.

children.label: A
children.text: shall revise the duties of the Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee to include identifying emerging and foundational technologies that may be developed over a period of 5 years or 10 years; and

children.label: i
children.text: the ownership by foreign persons and foreign governments of such technologies;

children.label: ii
children.text: the types of transactions related to such technologies engaged in by foreign persons and foreign governments;

children.label: iii
children.text: the blending of private and government investment in such technologies; and

children.label: iv
children.text: efforts to obfuscate ownership of such technologies or to otherwise circumvent the controls established under this section.

children.heading: Frequency
children.label: A Frequency
children.text: The Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee should meet not less frequently than every 120 days.

children.heading: Attendance
children.label: B Attendance
children.text: A representative from each agency participating in the interagency process established under subsection (a) should be in attendance at each meeting of the Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Classified information
children.label: 4 Classified information
children.text: Not fewer than half of the members of the Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee should hold sufficient security clearances such that classified information, including classified information described in clauses (ii) and (iii) of subsection (a)(2)(A), from the interagency process established under subsection (a) can be shared with those members to inform the advice provided by the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Applicability of Federal Advisory Committee Act
children.label: 5 Applicability of Federal Advisory Committee Act
children.text: Subsections (a)(1), (a)(3), and (b) of section 10 and sections 11, 13, and 14 of the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.) shall not apply to the Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Report
children.label: 6 Report
children.text: The Emerging Technology and Research Advisory Committee shall include the findings of the Advisory Committee under this subsection in the annual report to Congress required by .section 4824 of this title
